Steve Vinter

← Recipes

Braised Coconut Chickpeas

with Turmeric & Kale

SERVINGS: 2(4) | COOK TIME: 30(40) MINS | THIS ITEM IS SPICY 🌶

Remember to wash all produce.

Ingredients

  • ½ cup white quinoa
  • 1 red onion, peeled and diced
  • 2 garlic cloves, minced
  • 2 tsp fresh ginger, peeled and minced
  • 5.5 oz coconut milk
  • 1 Not-Chick'n™ Bouillon Cube
  • 4 oz curly kale, roughly chopped
  • 1 zucchini, diced
  • 13.4 oz chickpeas, drained and rinsed
  • 1 tsp ground turmeric
  • 0.25 oz fresh mint, leaves picked
  • 2 tsp red chile flakes 🌶

From your kitchen

  • 1 tbsp olive oil
  • Salt and pepper

Tools

  • Small saucepan with lid
  • Large pot

Ingredients are listed for 2 servings. If you're making 4 servings, please double your ingredients by using both meal kit bags provided.

Directions

  1. Cook the quinoa — Add quinoa, 1 cup water, and a pinch of salt to a small saucepan. Bring to a boil, cover, reduce heat to low, and cook until spirals burst and water is absorbed, 12 to 15 minutes. (4-serving meal: use 2 cups water)

  2. Cook the aromatics — Heat 1 tbsp olive oil in a large pot over medium-high heat. Add onion and a pinch of salt and pepper and cook until onion is softened, 3 to 4 minutes. Add garlic and ginger and cook until fragrant, 1 to 2 minutes. (4-serving meal: use 2 tbsp olive oil)

  3. Make the chickpea curry — Add coconut milk, ½ cup water, and bouillon cube to the pot with the aromatics and bring to a simmer. Add kale, zucchini, chickpeas, and turmeric. Stir the coconut chickpeas and cover. Cook until vegetables are bright green and tender, 4 to 5 minutes. Season to taste with salt. (4-serving meal: use 1 cup water)

  4. Serve — Divide the quinoa between bowls and ladle the braised coconut chickpeas on top. Sprinkle with mint and just ¼ tsp red chile flakes. Tuck in! (4-serving meal: use ½ tsp red chile flakes) TIP: Add more red chile flakes if you prefer more spice.

Nutrition

Nutrition per serving (as packaged) Calories: 700, Fat: 26 g, Sodium: 960 mg, Carbohydrates: 83 g, Protein: 28 g.

Allergens: tree nut (coconut). Manufactured on equipment shared with tree nuts, peanut, sesame, wheat, soy, egg, milk, fish, and shellfish.

Recipe from Purple Carrot.
